events.title: UN chief strongly condemns killing of another peacekeeper in southern Lebanon
events.summary: A UN peacekeeper has been killed and three others injured after a UNIFIL patrol came under fire in southern Lebanon, the mission said on Saturday.

events.title: UNICEF ‘outraged’ by killing of Gaza water truck drivers, urges investigation
events.summary: The UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) has raised alarm over the killing of two contracted workers delivering clean water to families in the Gaza Strip.

events.title: World News in Brief: Myanmar amnesty, rising needs in Afghanistan, another power loss at Ukraine nuclear plant
events.summary: Authorities in Myanmar released the country’s ousted president from prison on Friday, along with some 4,000 other people, as part of an amnesty to mark the traditional New Year festival.

events.title: ‘Slaughtered like goats’: Despair and abandonment in South Sudan
events.summary: Senior UN officials painted a sorry picture of South Sudan on Friday at the Security Council, describing political turmoil, rising violence, hunger and disease, amid budget cuts that are limiting the ability of the UN peacekeeping mission...

events.title: As the ‘world court’ turns 80, Guterres says law must prevail over force
events.summary: The world is at a “moment of crisis” and countries must reaffirm commitment to international law amid rising violations and geopolitical tensions, UN Secretary-General António Guterres said at an event on Friday to commemorate the 80th ann...

events.title: Record number of Rohingya refugees died at sea in 2025: UNHCR
events.summary: In 2025, nearly 900 Rohingya refugees were reported missing or dead in the Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal, making it the deadliest year on record in South and Southeast Asia, the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, said on Friday.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 April: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins; Iran announces opening of Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon came into effect at midnight in Beirut, offering a pause in hostilities after weeks of fighting. The UN Secretary-General has welcomed the agreement and urged all parties to respect it, express...

events.title: Deadly Russian strikes on Ukrainians ‘simply trying to live their lives’ must stop, says senior UN official
events.summary: The latest wave of Russian attacks on Ukrainian civilians in Dnipro, Kyiv and Odesa, which killed and injured scores of civilians, was roundly condemned by Matthias Schmale, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country.

events.title: UN peacekeeping missions under strain as funding cuts and new threats grow
events.summary: From election support in the Central African Republic to patrols in the disputed Abyei region, UN peacekeepers are operating in increasingly volatile environments – but shrinking resources and new threats, including drone warfare, are test...

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 16 April: UN meets over China-Russia Hormuz crisis veto as humanitarian crisis continues in Lebanon
events.summary: War in Lebanon continues amid the fragile US-Iran ceasefire amid hopes for direct talks between Israel and Beirut. Meanwhile, the UN General Assembly is debating the China-Russia Security Council veto on the crucial Strait of Hormuz crisis...

events.title: ‘Sudan is an atrocities laboratory’, UN aid chief tells Berlin conference
events.summary: Foreign ministers from across the world met in Berlin on Wednesday to show support for Sudan, where a fourth year of brutal warfare has begun as humanitarian needs deepen.

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Security fears rise in eastern DR Congo
events.summary: The UN Security Council met Wednesday over the deteriorating security situation in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and wider Great Lakes region. Despite mediation efforts in Doha and Washington, regional tensions between the DRC, Rwan...

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 15 April: Civilian dangers intensify as Israel expands Lebanon evacuation orders
events.summary: Across the Middle East, tensions remain high with Israeli military operations continuing in Lebanon following direct talks between the two governments in Washington on Tuesday. More than 2,000 Lebanese have been reported killed since early...

events.title: World failing Sudan as war enters a fourth year, UN relief chief warns
events.summary: Sudan remains the world’ s largest humanitarian and displacement crisis, UN agencies and partners said on Tuesday, calling for an end to the war between rival militaries on the eve of the three-year mark.

events.title: Yemenis are ‘hanging by a thread’ top aid official warns Security Council
events.summary: Yemen must not be drawn into the escalating conflict in the Middle East, the Security Council heard on Tuesday, stressing the need for de-escalation, political progress and urgent humanitarian funding for beleaguered civilians battered by...

events.title: ‘Time for diplomacy over escalation’ in Middle East war: Guterres
events.summary: As the war in the Middle East continues, the United Nations Secretary-General issued a passionate call for “serious negotiations” between the US and Iran to resume, warning that respect for international law “is being trampled” underfoot.

events.title: ‘Now is the moment to invest’: Syria needs global backing, says UN expert
events.summary: Syria has made “remarkable progress” on transitional justice within the past year, raising hopes for accountability and recovery after more than a decade of civil conflict.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 14 April: UN chief says US-Iran ceasefire must continue, urges respect for international law
events.summary: Respect for international law is being “trampled” the UN chief warned on Tuesday, urging further negotiations between the US and Iran over ending the war and extending the fragile ceasefire as needed. Saying “there is no military solution,...

events.title: Event report: Exploring the potential and applications of citizen science in areas affected by armed conflicts
events.summary: Elaine Donderer reports on the first in-person event of the European Citizen Science Association’s new working group on citizen science in areas affected by armed conflicts, which took place at the Association's 2026 conference at the Univ...

events.title: Sudan: Top UN aid official warns of ‘abandoned crisis’ as war enters fourth year
events.summary: As Sudan’s war moves into a fourth year, civilians are still being killed, displaced and subjected to widespread sexual violence, the UN’s top humanitarian official in the country warned on Monday, calling for urgent action to stop the fig...

events.title: US-Iran war: Guterres calls for continued talks, end to ceasefire violations
events.summary: The United States and Iran must continue talks aimed at ending their weeks-long war, UN Secretary-General António Guterres said on Monday, underscoring that there is no military solution to the conflict.

events.title: ‘Clock is ticking’: Hormuz disruption raises fears of global food crisis
events.summary: The clock is ticking for global food systems as disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz threaten to choke off the flow of fuel and crucial fertilizers needed for the next planting season – also raising the risk of higher food prices and a new...

events.title: European Union support for the UN ‘more vital than ever’, Security Council hears
events.summary: A senior United Nations official on Monday hailed the European Union (EU) as “a major economic and diplomatic actor and a strong advocate of multilateralism.”

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 13 April: US-Iran talks falter; Strait of Hormuz uncertainty deepens
events.summary: Weekend developments have raised fresh concerns worldwide after talks between the United States and Iran on Saturday ended without agreement, fuelling uncertainty and raising tensions once again. The planned US blockade targeting Iranian p...
